title: Proofpoint URL Defense User Click Risk (Allowed vs Blocked)
app: TA-proofpoint / Proofpoint Email Security parsing (org deployment)
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:click" earliest=-24h
| eval verdict=lower(coalesce(verdict, Verdict, ""))
| eval action=lower(coalesce(action, Action, disposition, ""))
| where verdict="malicious"
| stats count(eval(action="allowed" OR action="permitted")) as permitted_clicks count as total_clicks by userPrincipalName, url
| eval permit_rate=round(100*permitted_clicks/nullif(total_clicks,0),1)
| where permitted_clicks>0
| sort -permitted_clicks
description: For malicious URLs, comparing permitted versus blocked clicks shows users who bypassed browser protections or policy exceptions—prime candidates for containment and training.
value: Separates noisy total click volume from high-risk permitted clicks on known-bad URLs, closing the gap between gateway detection and endpoint compromise.
implementation: Map `userPrincipalName` from your click feed—some deployments use `user` or `recipient`. Align `action` vocabulary (`allowed`, `permitted`, `not blocked`). Integrate with IdP username fields for password resets. Respect privacy policies on URL storage.
visualization: Table (user × URL × permitted count), bar chart (top users), timeline.

references.title: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk
